zone_6b_notes: Missouri native — extremely reliable, long-lived perennial in Zone 6b. Blooms June through September in Farmington. Drought tolerant once established; thrives in Missouri heat. Leave seed heads standing through winter — goldfinches and other birds feed on them heavily. Divide every 4–5 years to maintain vigor. Deadhead spent blooms to encourage rebloom, or leave some for self-seeding. Magnus (large blooms, classic pink-purple) and Kim's Knee High (compact, front of border) are excellent cultivars. Attracts monarchs, swallowtails, bees, and goldfinches all season.

planting.best_planting_time: Direct sow in fall for spring germination; transplant spring or fall
